hero.name: Benjamin Jarlsson Nemeth
hero.description: I build systems that connect engineering, software, and intelligent behavior. My focus is on understanding how systems work at a deep level and designing solutions that are both structured and practical.

sections.title: Who am I?
sections.text: I’m an Electrical Engineering student at DTU with a strong interest in how systems are built from the ground up. I am naturally curious about how components interact, how data flows through systems, and how decisions are made within constraints. I enjoy working across disciplines — combining hardware, software, and structured thinking into systems that actually function in practice. My approach is hands-on, iterative, and focused on understanding rather than just completing tasks.

sections.title: What I Build
sections.text: I work on small to medium-scale projects that connect theory with implementation. These projects often involve combining different technologies — such as backend systems, simple frontends, and logical architectures — into one cohesive solution. While not all projects are large, they are all intentional and built to deepen my understanding of system behavior, structure, and design. I focus on learning through building, testing, and refining.

sections.title: How I Work
sections.text: I approach problems by first trying to understand the overall system before diving into details. I prefer building simple versions quickly, testing them, and then iterating. This allows me to learn fast and adapt my approach based on real results rather than assumptions. I value clarity, structure, and efficiency, both in code and in system design. I aim to create solutions that are not only functional, but also logically organized and easy to extend.

sections.title: Technical Direction
sections.text: My current focus is on becoming strong at the intersection of hardware and software. I am particularly interested in embedded systems, system-level engineering, and how intelligent behavior can be integrated into real systems. Over time, I aim to work on more advanced systems that involve real-time processing, decision-making, and interaction with the physical world. I am still exploring, but always with the goal of building a solid and flexible foundation.

sections.title: About
sections.text: This section goes deeper into my background, how I think, what drives me, and the direction I am working toward. It covers both my technical development and my personal approach to learning and problem solving. If you want to understand how I approach engineering beyond just tools and projects, this is the place to start.

sections.title: Projects
sections.text: My projects are focused on exploration and learning. They are not always large, but they are designed to test ideas, connect concepts, and build practical understanding. You will find a mix of software systems, structured applications, and technical experiments that reflect my current level and direction.

sections.title: What I’m Looking For
sections.text: I am looking for opportunities where I can learn, contribute, and work with real systems. I am especially interested in environments where engineering is both practical and structured — where I can develop my understanding of hardware, software, and system design in real-world contexts. I value environments where curiosity, responsibility, and continuous improvement are part of the culture.

sections.title: Why Contact?
sections.text: Feel free to reach out if you want to collaborate, discuss ideas, or connect around engineering, systems, or technology. I am always open to conversations about projects, technical challenges, or opportunities where I can learn and contribute. Even simple discussions often lead to new perspectives and ideas.

sections.title: Core Focus
items: AI and intelligent behavior in systems

items.label: Present
items.title: DTU — Electrical Engineering
items.description: Currently studying Electrical Engineering with a focus on building strong technical foundations. Working with both theoretical and practical aspects of engineering, including circuits, systems, and structured problem solving. Continuously applying knowledge through projects and independent exploration.

items.label: 2024–Present
items.title: Technical Work — Ryde Technologies
items.description: Gaining hands-on experience with real hardware systems, focusing on diagnostics, repair, and system reliability. Learning how systems behave outside ideal conditions and developing structured troubleshooting approaches.

items.title: Academic Foundation — St. Olav / UiS
items.description: Built a strong base in mathematics, physics, and technical subjects. Developed analytical thinking and an interest in engineering systems, which led to pursuing Electrical Engineering.

items.label: Ongoing
items.title: Independent Exploration
items.description: Continuously working on small projects and technical experiments to expand my understanding. Focused on connecting theory with practice and exploring new technologies.

items.title: DTU — Electrical Engineering (Ongoing)
items.description: Continuing to build strong technical skills and understanding of engineering systems, combining theory and practical work across multiple domains.

items.title: Top Grades — Technical Courses
items.description: Achieved top grades in demanding technical and mathematical courses, demonstrating strong analytical thinking and consistent performance.

items.title: Independent Projects
items.description: Developed multiple small-scale systems and applications, focusing on structured design, implementation, and learning through practice.

items.title: Technical Work Experience
